title: DASH Q4 Deep Dive: Growth in Non-Restaurant Verticals and Tech Investments Shape Outlook
text: DoorDash's Q4 2024 results highlighted significant growth in non-restaurant verticals and substantial technology investments shaping the company's future outlook. Over 25% of monthly active users now order from non-restaurant categories like groceries and retail, compared to 20% in 2023, demonstrating successful diversification execution. The company is investing several hundred million dollars more in new initiatives and platform development in 2026 compared to 2025 levels, targeting new product development and global technology platform rollout. Management outlined a global technology stack rollout to expand the company's capabilities across international markets. The advertising business emerged as a key driver of margin expansion, with net revenue margin reaching 13.8% in Q3 2025, up from 13.5% in prior quarters. Higher-margin revenue streams including advertising, subscriptions, and delivery services for third-party merchants are becoming increasingly important to overall profitability. The company expects unit economics in its grocery and retail categories to turn positive in 2H 2026, validating the long-term strategy. DoorDash's dominance in U.S. markets and expanding international presence provide substantial growth runway. The company remains focused on balancing near-term profitability with long-term market share expansion and new market development.
